name: SpinQuant
oneliner: Learns optimal rotation matrices during training so that rotated weights are easier to quantize — a QAT approach using geometric transformations.
explanation: Similar insight to QuIP# (rotations make weights more quantization-friendly), but instead of random rotations, SpinQuant LEARNS the optimal rotations during a fine-tuning phase. This means the model actively optimizes its internal representations to be amenable to low-bit quantization. Combines the geometric insight of incoherence processing with the training-time adaptation of QAT. Published by Meta researchers.
fundamentals: During fine-tuning: insert learnable rotation matrices R before quantization in each layer. Forward pass: W' = R @ W, then quantize W'. Gradients flow through to both W and R via STE. The rotations R converge to transformations that spread weight information evenly, minimizing quantization error.

foundational_papers.title: SpinQuant: LLM Quantization with Learned Rotations
